meaning: adj.旋转的,眩晕的,眼花的,迅速变化的,不稳定的
examples: From the path there was a vertiginous drop to the valley below.
First the shock of love with its vertiginous rush and the sweet fire in his spine.
The announcement threw the emirate and the global credit markets into a vertiginous free fall.
What struck me more than the cold August fog was the vertiginous rise in rents.
To the south, Patagonia is a land of windswept steppes, vertiginous mountains and massive glaciers.
